Hedera helix var. marginata, also known as Canary Island ivy or Hedera marginata, is a variety of the English ivy plant (Hedera helix). Hedera marginata is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress, and support immune function.

The bioactive constituents of Hedera marginata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to the plant's potential health benefits.

Hedera marginata is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to support digestive health, reduce stress, and promote relaxation. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these preparations.
